claim: The 'lethal trifecta' (private data access + untrusted input + exfiltration vector) makes agents inherently risky; all three must be present for exploit
actionable: Design agent systems to limit at least one pillar: minimize private data access, block untrusted inputs, or prevent exfiltration; reduce surface area intentionally
source_title: Full Guide - Build Your Own AI Second Brain with Claude Code

claim: Building custom agent systems over generic platforms gives engineers control over permission boundaries and lets them start with zero-trust, adding capabilities gradually
actionable: Build your agent runtime instead of using off-the-shelf platforms; start with read-only APIs and add write/execute permission only where needed
source_title: Full Guide - Build Your Own AI Second Brain with Claude Code

claim: A memory layer (soul.md, user.md, memory.md) powering every conversation ensures agents become more aligned with user goals over time
actionable: Implement a persistent memory system in markdown; load it into every agent conversation; update it daily with lessons and decisions
source_title: Full Guide - Build Your Own AI Second Brain with Claude Code

claim: Daily reflection loops (extracting key decisions and lessons from raw logs) allow agents to learn from every interaction without token bloat
actionable: After every agent session, run a compaction job that extracts key facts into a memory file; index raw logs for RAG when needed
source_title: Full Guide - Build Your Own AI Second Brain with Claude Code

claim: Skills for specialized tasks (generating diagrams, writing YouTube scripts, managing tasks) become more effective when they access a shared memory layer
actionable: Design skills to reference the shared memory layer; this allows specialized skills to be more personalized without requiring task-specific fine-tuning
source_title: Full Guide - Build Your Own AI Second Brain with Claude Code

claim: A heartbeat mechanism (periodic agent invocation with contextual APIs) enables proactive agent behavior without continuous prompting
actionable: Schedule periodic agent runs (e.g., every 30 mins) with pre-gathered API context (emails, tasks, calendar); let agent reason about what to do
source_title: Full Guide - Build Your Own AI Second Brain with Claude Code

claim: Starting with a zero-trust permission model and expanding gradually is more sustainable than revoking capabilities after misuse
actionable: Begin by giving agents read-only access to APIs; require explicit manual approval before granting write or execute permissions
source_title: Full Guide - Build Your Own AI Second Brain with Claude Code
